OMI_Receipt (C struct).valid: int — must be 1 for verifiable receipt

candidate_receipt_fields.title: declaration title
candidate_receipt_fields.criteria_met: number of criteria met out of 9

receipt_id.gap: No runtime equivalent — receipt_id is a registry-layer concept
receipt_id.action: Keep as registry index. Not lowered to runtime.

sid.runtime_equivalent: none (runtime uses envelope bytes, not SIDs)
sid.gap: Runtime operates on 512-bit envelopes, not symbolic SIDs
sid.action: Map sid → envelope identity during lowering. Not yet implemented.

scope.gs.gap: Runtime resolves declaration layer from envelope bytes, not from scope.gs
scope.gs.action: scope.gs is a classification hint, not a runtime value. Compatible.

adjudication.gap: Runtime issues receipts on state transitions. Adjudication is a pre-runtime concept.
adjudication.action: Map promote-candidate → Pending. Runtime acceptance happens downstream.

promotion_class.runtime_equivalent: none (runtime does not classify promotion eligibility)
promotion_class.gap: Promotion eligibility is a build-pass concept, not a runtime concept
promotion_class.action: Keep as build-pass metadata. Not lowered to runtime.

criteria_detail.runtime_equivalent: none (runtime does not track build criteria)
criteria_detail.gap: Build criteria are build-pass metadata, not runtime state
criteria_detail.action: Keep as build-pass metadata. Not lowered to runtime.

lowerable_fields: scope.fs/gs/rs/us → can map to Omicron layer resolution
kind → can map to envelope classification
adjudication → maps to receipt lifecycle state
gaps: Runtime expects 512-bit envelope, not symbolic SID
Runtime computes Omicron layers from envelope bytes, not from scope fields
Runtime issues receipts on state transitions, not on promotion eligibility
Runtime receipt is 64-bit packed, not JSON
Lowering MUST NOT create receipts (per LOWERING.md)
compatibility.caveat: Candidate receipts are build-pass records, not runtime receipts. They must be lowered through the OMI-Lisp → Compiler → ISA pipeline before they become runtime receipts. The current schema is compatible as an upstream record but must undergo lowering to become runtime receipts.
